content: Just as learning the vocabulary entry for 'reflexion' in high school English curriculum teaches students to explicitly map internal thought processes into coherent language to refine understanding, the LLM reflexion agent can borrow this structured vocabulary-level scaffolding to break down vague self-reflection outputs into graded, learnable reflection 'vocabulary' of common error types to improve its self-correction ability. This creates a human learning framework for LLM self-reflection that turns abstract self-criticism into structured skill-building.
